steps.title: Focus on a sculpture
steps.content: The student's goal is narrow: the sentence must provide an example of one of Catlett's sculptures. The notes include both kinds of work by Catlett, so the best choice has to name a specific artwork and identify it as a sculpture rather than a print, a broad description of her career, or a biographical fact.

steps.title: Remove the wrong details
steps.content:
• Choice A - 'The print Dancing was created by celebrated artist Eleanor Catlett in 1990.': This names a specific work, but the notes identify Dancing as a print, so it does not provide an example of a sculpture.
  • Choice B - 'Artist Eleanor Catlett is best known for creating sculptures and prints that explore the Black experience.': This summarizes Catlett's broader work but does not name any specific sculpture.
  • Choice D - 'Eleanor Catlett, a celebrated artist, was born in 1915.': This gives biographical information only and does not name an artwork.
The remaining choice uses the notes to name Star Gazer, identifies it as a sculpture, and gives its year. The answer is C.
